entry.title: Rotorua
entry.content:
Rotorua, New Zealand.


These are some of the most active geo-thermals on the planet. The geyser on the center (Lady Knox) is the tallest in southern hemisphere. Oh there was a beautiful rainbow while it was irrupting. How temperamental, intriguing, and wonderful at the same time. In the evening, we had dinner at Te Puia Maori village. Meals were cooked by keeping under the volcanic rocks.

entry.title: Hobbiton
entry.content:
Hobbiton, Matamata, New Zealand.


Roads Go Ever On

Roads go ever ever on,
Over rock and under tree,
By caves where never sun has shone,
By streams that never find the sea;
Over snow by winter sown,
And through the merry flowers of June,
Over grass and over stone,
And under mountains in the moon.

Roads go ever ever on,
Under cloud and under star.
Yet feet that wandering have gone
Turn at last to home afar.
Eyes that fire and sword have seen,
And horror in the halls of stone
Look at last on meadows green,
And trees and hills they long have known.

The Road goes ever on and on
Down from the door where it began.
Now far ahead the Road has gone,
And I must follow, if I can,
Pursuing it with eager feet,
Until it joins some larger way,
Where many paths and errands meet.
And whither then? I cannot say.

The Road goes ever on and on
Out from the door where it began.
Now far ahead the Road has gone.
Let others follow, if they can!
Let them a journey new begin.
But I at last with weary feet
Will turn towards the lighted inn,
My evening-rest and sleep to meet.

— J.R.R. Tolkien, The Lord of the Rings.

entry.title: Huka falls
entry.content:
Huka falls, Taupō, New Zealand.

Perhaps, you have heard about how kind the Kiwis are. Here is one of my first hand experiences. We spend this morning in Craters of Moon National Park. Later we planned to walk from there to Huka falls. The weather was really volatile. When we were about to leave the park, it started pouring. So we decided to call a taxi from the city. While we were waiting, a park employee came to us and asked where we were heading. He told he finished his shift and going back to the city, so if we want he can give us a ride. We said but we are going to another point, he said that is fine, he would drop us there. But we confirmed the taxi, and the driver perhaps already left from the city. He insisted it is absolutely fine to cancel. We called back to cancel, nobody argued or asked for penalty :P So we got on his car, he told us some stories along the way. He dropped us, wished good luck, and we could never thank him enough.

Few more clicks on our way back to Lake Taupō along the river.

entry.title: Waiheke Island
entry.content: Waiheke is about one hour by boat from Auckland Central. The ferry arrives at Matiatia Wharf. Then intra-island busses can be used to move around or just walk. You have to plan which points you are going to cover; seeing each and every corner is not feasible in a single day. There are several beaches, beautiful Oneroa is one of them.

entry.title: Pulau Ubin
entry.content:
Pulau Ubin is northeast of mainland Singapore. We took a morning ferry from Changi Ferry Terminal. Got to the island and rented bikes to move around the island. There are restaurants, no need to worry about survival. The island is big enough to spend a whole day. Tropical forest, mangroves, coconut trees, some sand beaches, small lakes. There are some watchtowers for panoramic views. Depending on the timing, you might be able to see some beautiful coral reefs. If it is high-tide, they might be underwater. Birds, monkeys, crabs, lizards and mosquitoes all around. Don’t forget to bring insect repellant and wear full sleeve. It also lines up with one of Changi landing runway, you will see airplanes descending very low every couple of minutes.

Individual pics on 500px

entry.title: Napolionica to Duino walk
entry.content:
We had a long Easter walk today. Starting from Opicina Obelisk to Duino.

More pics: http://bit.ly/duino-walk

entry.title: A trail walk at Val Rosandra

link.title: A trail walk at Val Rosandra

entry.content: We had an enchanting trail walk at Val Rosandra. It was a sunny day. There were nice panoramic views, with mountains in our left and right, and in front and in back too. We walked along the river Val Rosandra, and occasionally crossing back and forth. The water stream made some wonderful cavities over the years. Then there was a tall beautiful waterfall. At the end we had some refreshments at a home-like restaurant. We had drinks, salami with cheese, olives, and warm breads. It was a wonderful day deep in the nature and with good friends :)

entry.title: Good evening Trieste
entry.content: Sight of Trieste from Napoleonica after the sun went down in this northern Italian town. In fact, it’s bird’s eye view of three countries: Italia, Slovenia, and Croatia. The bright lights remind me of Diwali (the famous festival of light in India).

entry.title: Barcolana 2015
entry.content:
Today was the 47th Barcolana. About 1700 sailing boats participated in today’s sailing festival. This is the most crowded sailing competition I have ever seen, maybe you remember the scene from the movie Troy. It was a magnificent event. I had a wonderful sight from Napoleonica, which is high in the mountains. There was a lot of crowd everywhere, in Opicina, Piazza Unita, Barcola, to watch the event. The winner of the competition was Robertissima III.

More pics on 500px.

entry.title: Bárcola, Trieste
entry.content: A sunset from Bárcola on the other day! Taken on my phone while running.
